doc_title: On the Impact of Outlier Bias on User Clicks

items.fact: Outliers are items that stand out in the ranking w.r.t. observable item features.
items.evidence_span: We follow the definition of outliers in ranking from [34]: outliers are items that stand out in the ranking w.r.t. observable item features.

items.fact: Users' implicit feedback, such as clicks, can be a valuable source of supervision for CLTR.
items.evidence_span: Users' implicit feedback, such as clicks, can be a valuable source of supervision for CLTR [2].

items.fact: Presentation bias considers a related phenomenon; items with bold keywords in their titles appear more attractive.
items.evidence_span: Presentation bias [46] considers a related phenomenon; items with bold keywords in their titles appear more attractive.

items.fact: Context bias in news-feed recommendation affects CTR differently than outlier bias.
items.evidence_span: The closest concept to our work is context bias in news-feed recommendation [44]; CTR is lower for products when surrounded by at least one very similar product than when surrounded by non-similar products.

items.fact: Unbiased learning to rank approaches train an unbiased ranking model directly with biased user feedback.
items.evidence_span: Unbiased learning to rank approaches train an unbiased ranking model directly with biased user feedback [7].

items.fact: Fatemeh Sarvi et al. 2022. Understanding and Mitigating the Effect of Outliers in Fair Ranking. In WSDM. 861-869.
entities: Understanding and Mitigating the Effect of Outliers in Fair Ranking
items.evidence_span: [34] Fatemeh Sarvi, Maria Heuss, Mohammad Aliannejadi, Sebastian Schelter, and Maarten de Rijke. 2022. Understanding and Mitigating the Effect of Outliers in Fair Ranking. In WSDM. 861-869.

items.fact: Qingyao Ai et al. 2021. Unbiased Learning to Rank: Online or Offline? ACM Transactions on Information Systems (TOIS) 39, 2 (2021), 1-29.
entities: Unbiased Learning to Rank: Online or Offline?
items.evidence_span: [7] Qingyao Ai, Tao Yang, Huazheng Wang, and Jiaxin Mao. 2021. Unbiased Learning to Rank: Online or Offline? ACM Transactions on Information Systems (TOIS) 39, 2 (2021), 1-29.

items.fact: Sarvi et al. [34] show that outlier items receive more attention from users.
items.evidence_span: Sarvi et al. [34] show that outlier items receive more attention from users.
items.relevance: citation to 'Understanding and Mitigating the Effect of Outliers in Fair Ranking'
